claim: Traditional CI/CD designed for 1-2 human diffs/week breaks at agent scale with thousands of PRs creating cache thrash and merge queue bottlenecks
actionable: Replace PR-based CI/CD with intent+plan fed to agent loops with fast inline validation and pre-merge queue
source_title: CI/CD Is Dead, Agents Need Continuous Compute and Computers — Hugo Santos and Madison Faulkner

claim: New architecture removes PRs entirely - uses intent/plan, agent loop for implementation, internal validation (build/test), external validation (other agents), pre-merge queue for reconciliation
actionable: Implement agent-first development: codify intent, loop agents on plan with internal tests, validate with agent checkers, use pre-merge queue for human review
source_title: CI/CD Is Dead, Agents Need Continuous Compute and Computers — Hugo Santos and Madison Faulkner

claim: At agent scale, statefulness of agent environment is critical - don't start from scratch each iteration as it delays loops exponentially
actionable: Maintain persistent agent execution environments with memory across iterations to avoid re-computation overhead
source_title: CI/CD Is Dead, Agents Need Continuous Compute and Computers — Hugo Santos and Madison Faulkner

claim: Future systems may have agents exploring multiple commits in parallel for same plan in 'multiverse' mode where repo tip is moving target
actionable: Design agent systems to support branching/merging of parallel agent work streams with eventual consistency
source_title: CI/CD Is Dead, Agents Need Continuous Compute and Computers — Hugo Santos and Madison Faulkner
